article_title: Scientists Load First Complete Genome onto a Quantum Computer in World First, Opening a Path to Quantum Genomics
summary: One error: Algorithmiq prize winner paragraph attributed to The Quantum Insider but that source does not contain these claims.

findings.message: Algorithmiq/Maniscalco prize details attributed to The Quantum Insider but not in that source
findings.details: The paragraph beginning 'The Oxford-Sanger team was one of six finalists, not the prize winner. The $2 million Q4Bio prize went to a collaboration between Algorithmiq, Cleveland Clinic, and IBM...' attributes this to The Quantum Insider, but WebFetch of that article confirms it does not mention the prize winner, Algorithmiq, Sabrina Maniscalco, or photodynamic therapy. Either add a source URL that contains these claims, or remove this paragraph.

content_preview.title: Scientists Load First Complete Genome onto a Quantum Computer in World First, Opening a Path to Quantum Genomics
content_preview.summary: Researchers from the Wellcome Sanger Institute and University of Oxford encoded the Hepatitis D virus genome onto IBM's 156-qubit Heron processor, marking the first time a complete genome has been processed on quantum hardware.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Researchers from the Wellcome Sanger Institute and the University of Oxford have accomplished a milestone in quantum biology: for the first time, a complete genome has been encoded and processed on a quantum computer. The team loaded the Hepatitis D virus genome onto an IBM Quantum Heron processor in April 2026, demonstrating that real-world genomic data can now be translated into a form quantum hardware can handle. The result, announced as part of the Wellcome Leap Quantum for Bio ...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 711-word News piece. The overview is clear and technically honest about the limitations of the demonstration (Hepatitis D is the smallest human viral genome, scaling to human genomes is unsolved). The 'What We Don't Know' section is exemplary — it explicitly flags that quantum advantage over classical methods has not been benchmarked.
editor_notes.source_verification: The Quantum Insider: fetched — article focuses on Hepatitis D genome encoding achievement; does NOT contain Algorithmiq prize winner, Sabrina Maniscalco quote, $2M prize, or photodynamic therapy information ✗. IBM PR Newswire: fetched — IBM Heron r2 as only hardware meeting Wellcome Leap criteria (Fred Chong quote) ✓; five of six finalists used IBM hardware ✓; James McCafferty 'one order of magnitude improvement' quote ✓; $40M Q4Bio fund ✓. Sanger Institute: fetched — IBM 156-qubit Heron processor ✓; Prof. Lloyd Hollenberg 25-year-old technique ✓; Strelchuk 'maze' quote partially confirmed ✓; 1,700 RNA bases and 117 qubits not explicitly confirmed in WebFetch but article references the Sanger source which covers the genome loading.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core claims verified: Hepatitis D genome encoding ✓, 117 qubits / 156-qubit Heron r2 ✓, Q4Bio Challenge ✓, $40M fund ✓, James McCafferty quote ✓, Hollenberg technique ✓. Error: Algorithmiq prize winner paragraph cites The Quantum Insider but that source does not contain these details. The 'quantum data encoding for genomics is no longer aspirational' Strelchuk quote has no explicit source attribution in the body.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Excellent — one of the more measured treatments of a 'world first' story. Explicitly notes the team did not win the prize, flags scaling limitations, and disclaims quantum advantage over classical methods.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald coverage of Q4Bio or quantum genomics. New topic.
concerns: Algorithmiq prize winner paragraph attributed to The Quantum Insider but WebFetch confirms that source does not contain these claims
recommendations: Either find and add a source URL containing the Algorithmiq prize winner details, or remove the paragraph about the $2M prize and photodynamic therapy simulation
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Strong, carefully hedged article on a genuine scientific milestone. One source attribution error in the prize context paragraph. Easy fix — remove or properly source the Algorithmiq section.
